rulings.Case Title: MELVIN BLAIR VS. BRUCE MCCORMACK MD et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 10, 2013, Line 8, DEFENDANT MICHELLE CUPA-HOFER, R.N.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrer is overruled. Defendants' demurrer is overruled. The 1 year statute of limitation under CCP § 340.5 commenced on January 3, 2011, when Plaintiff first became aware of the negligent cause of his injury - a sponge had been left in his back after surgery. (See Dolan v. Borelli, 13 Cal. App. 4th 816, 823 (1993) (citing Jolly v. Eli Lilly & Co. (1988) 44 Cal.3d 1103, 1109.).) Because Plaintiff sent the Notice of Claim on October 14, 2011, within 90 days of the expiration of the limitations period, an additional 90 days was added to the limitations period. (See Woods v. Young (1991) 53 Cal.3d 315, 325.) Following Camarillo v. Vaage (2003) 105 Cal.App.4th 552, 567, the statute of limitations is tolled 90 days against both known and unknown Defendants. Consequently, Plaintiff's original Complaint, filed January 26, 2012, was not time barred against Doe Defendants. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: RANDY LEE MCDOWELL VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And Related Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Wednesday, July 10, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFF RANDY MCDOWELL'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And Related Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The Court has not received courtesy copies of the opposition filed on June 26, 2013. Wells Fargo Bank is directed to provide an electronic copy of all papers filed in opposition to the motion to Pro Tem Judge Watson at jamesw@skwsf.com no later than July 9, 2013 at 4 pm. The opposing party shall also provide courtesy copies of the opposition at the hearing. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARIYA YUKHIMOVA VS. GOLDEN STATE ADULT DAY HEALTH CARE, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 10, 2013, Line 25, DEFENDANT GOLDEN STATE ADULT DAY HEALTH CARE, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. The motion for summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. Defendant fails to shift the burden on the individual causes of action. Defendant is liable for Mr. Scamin's conduct because defendant owed a non-delegable duty to plaintiff based upon 22 CCR 54309(a)(8) and defendant's contract with California. "If¿an individual or corporation undertakes to carry on an activity involving possible danger to the public under a license or franchise granted by public authority subject to certain obligations or liabilities imposed by the public authority, these liabilities may not be evaded by delegating performance to an independent contractor. The original contractor remains subject to liability for harm caused by the negligence of the independent contractor employed to do the work." Taylor v. Oakland Scavenger Co. (1941) 17 Cal.2d at 594, 604. The Court denies summary adjudication as to the claim for punitive damages. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant acted with a conscious disregard as to plaintiff's safety. Defendant fails to shift the burden with regard to whether a corporate officer, director, or managing agent engaged in the malicious conduct. That issue was first raised in the reply. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JON GRAY VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 10, 2013, Line 11, DEFENDANTs CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT, SAN FRANCISCO POLICE COMMISSION & CHIEF OF POLICE's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to allege an enforceable mandatory duty. The amended complaint fails to state a cause of action. SF Charter 4.127 does not require defendant to process plaintiff's application. The Charter's language is discretionary and simply states that the Commission may appoint patrol special police officers. Similarly, the Commission's Interim Rules and Procedures use discretionary language. See Sec. 1.04. Lastly, none of those rules is an "enactment" within the meaning of Government Code § 810.6. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ALEX SANCHEZ VS. BERGER, TATIANA INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE OF THE et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 10, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANTs tatiana BERGER & ZOJA JACOBS REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege a cause of action for breach of contract as Plaintiff alleges that he entered into a contract with all Defendants. (CCP § 430.10(e). See Complaint, 54.) However, Plaintiff concedes that Plaintiff did not specify whether the contract was entered into orally or in writing. (CCP § 430.10(g). See Opposition, p. 2:5-7.) Plaintiff is granted leave to amend the sixth cause of action, if Plaintiff can do so in good faith, to allege whether the contract was entered into orally or in writing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: O'HARA, BRIAN BY AND THROUGH HIS SUCCESSOR-IN- et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 10, 2013, Line 18, DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend as to the second cause of action, and overruled as to the first cause of action. On a demurrer, all facts pled in the complaint are assumed to be true, regardless of improbability. For the first cause of action, Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts with the requisite particularity to allege professional negligence against Defendant Regents of the University of California ("Defendant"). For the second cause of action, Plaintiff has not pled sufficient facts to allege elder or dependent abuse. The allegations of reckless, oppression, fraud and malice are conclusory. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend the second cause of action, if he can do so in good faith, to allege that Defendant's neglect of the Decedent was reckless or done with oppression, fraud or malice. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ABREHA KIDANE VS. ELMER TEP CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Waive Undertaking (Bond Or Cash) Requirement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 10, 2013, Line 24, APPELLANT ABREHA KIDANE's Motion To Waive Undertaking (Bond Or Cash) Requirement. Denied. Mr. Kidane has not sufficiently demonstrated indigence or inability to obtain sufficient sureties to waive the bond requirement of Labor Code § 98.2(b). (CCP § 995.240.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
